steps.action: **Locate** the Safari icon in the Dock
steps.targetDesc: Compass-style blue/white icon at the bottom of the screen
steps.expectedState: Safari icon becomes highlighted on hover
steps.why: The Dock is the fastest access point for frequently used apps
steps.pitfall: Don't confuse Safari with other browser icons (Chrome, Firefox)

steps.action: **Click** the Safari icon in the Dock
steps.targetDesc: Single-click on the blue compass icon
steps.expectedState: Safari bounces briefly and launches; a new browser window appears
steps.why: Single-click is all that's needed to open an app from the Dock
steps.pitfall: Avoid double-clicking — it may open two windows

steps.targetDesc: Look for the Safari menu bar at the top of the screen ("Safari", "File", "Edit", etc.)
steps.expectedState: Menu bar shows Safari-specific options; address bar is visible and active
steps.why: Confirms the app launched successfully, not just minimized
steps.pitfall: If nothing appears, check if the window opened behind other apps

steps.targetDesc: Keyboard shortcut anywhere on the desktop
steps.expectedState: Spotlight Search bar appears in the center of the screen
steps.why: Spotlight finds any app instantly without browsing folders
steps.pitfall: Make sure no text field is focused before pressing the shortcut

steps.expectedState: Safari appears as the top result with its compass icon
steps.why: Typing the app name is faster than navigating Finder
steps.pitfall: Avoid typing extra characters — Safari should appear after 3–4 letters

steps.expectedState: Safari launches and a new browser window opens
steps.why: Confirms and opens the top search result
steps.pitfall: Don't click elsewhere or Spotlight will dismiss without opening the app
